source_files_consulted: Kalshi position screenshot - LAL team total over 94.5

series_context.next_round: Lakers advance to face Oklahoma City Thunder
series_context.closeout_context: Houston entered with two straight wins after trailing 0-3, but missing Kevin Durant, Fred VanVleet and Steven Adams.

investment_results.selection: Yes - Los Angeles Lakers over 94.5 points

model_projection_vs_outcome.available_projection_data: No formal pregame sim mean/median recorded in chat for this game. LiveFlow identified LAL TT over 94.5 as an actionable in-game edge at +123.

drivers: Houston shot creation depleted by Durant and VanVleet inactive
Lakers controlled defensive glass and transition
LeBron usage and Rui spacing created stable LAL scoring floor
Houston perimeter shooting failure created one-sided game script

Q1.note: LAL survived early Houston energy and took control late in quarter.

Q2.note: Decisive quarter. Houston shot 5-22 FG, 0-4 from three, and committed 6 turnovers.

Q3.note: Houston stabilized with free throws, but still entered Q4 down 16.

Q4.note: LAL reopened control with a 10-3 start and cashed the live team total.

first_half.note: LAL held Houston to 29.3% FG and 18.2% from three before halftime.

rebound_environment_analysis.key_signal: LAL won the glass 54-45 despite Houston's big frontcourt minutes. Ayton controlled the defensive glass with 13 defensive rebounds and 16 total rebounds.
rebound_environment_analysis.houston_issue: Houston's miss volume was massive, but their offensive rebounding did not scale: only 8 offensive rebounds on 52 missed FG/FT events and 9 second-chance points.
rebound_environment_analysis.miss_profile: Houston's 5-28 three-point shooting created long-miss volume, but LAL guard/wing gang rebounding and Ayton's interior positioning prevented a Rockets crash avalanche.

LAL: LeBron logged 37:07 with +26 and controlled tempo through the closeout stretch.
Ayton played only 28:10 due to foul count but still produced 16 rebounds.
Rui played 34:44 and hit 5 of 7 threes, creating spacing that Houston could not punish.
HOU: Amen played 44:33 and Jabari 41:59; usage burden was high due to Durant and VanVleet inactive.
Sengun played 37:35 but had 4 turnovers and limited assist creation.
Reed Sheppard was forced into 36:29 and shot 1-10 from three, a major offensive failure point.

modify.reason: Houston's regular offensive-glass identity did not fully translate without Steven Adams and with Durant/VanVleet absent; offensive boards dropped to 8 despite massive miss volume.
Reed Sheppard's 1-10 three-point shooting and 0 rebounds show high fragility when elevated into major playoff creation minutes.

retraining_flags.description: LAL over 94.5 team total at +123 was locked at 81 points with 6:28 left and cashed at 98; validate late-entry TT overs when team has stable half-court initiator plus defensive control.
Houston without Durant and VanVleet produced only 78 points, 13 assists, and 5 made threes; missing top creators should apply stronger playoff scoring suppression.
Houston's offensive rebounding profile weakened without Adams and with heavy defensive attention on Sengun/Amen. Do not auto-carry regular-season OREB dominance into depleted playoff rotations.
